title: Schema Mapping Transformation
description: Demonstrates how a user query at external level gets transformed to physical storage access
explanation: Shows how DBMS transforms user queries through three-schema levels, using mapping definitions at each stage

title: Data Independence Demonstration
description: Shows how DBMS handles schema changes without affecting applications
explanation: Demonstrates Physical Data Independence - storage reorganization doesn't require application changes

title: Three-Schema Implementation Example
description: SQL examples showing different schema levels
code:
-- INTERNAL SCHEMA (Physical Storage)
-- How data is actually stored (DBMS dependent)
-- Index on student_id (B-tree)
-- Clustered index on marks
-- Data pages: 8KB each

-- CONCEPTUAL SCHEMA (Logical Structure)
CREATE TABLE Student (
 student_id VARCHAR(10) PRIMARY KEY,
 name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
 marks INTEGER,
 department_id VARCHAR(5),
 FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES Department(dept_id)
);

-- EXTERNAL SCHEMA (User Views)
-- Student view for exam department
CREATE VIEW ExamStudent AS
SELECT student_id, name, marks
FROM Student
WHERE marks IS NOT NULL;

-- Student view for admin department 
CREATE VIEW AdminStudent AS
SELECT student_id, name, department_id
FROM Student;
explanation: Shows how the same database can have different representations at each schema level
